info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing v2 API
info.description:
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing automatically distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets, such as Amazon EC2 instances, containers, IP addresses, and Lambda functions. It supports Application Load Balancers, Network Load Balancers, and Gateway Load Balancers.

Contact Support:
 Name: Amazon Web Services

item.name: ?Action=CreateLoadBalancer
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Load Balancer

query.description: (Required) The name of the load balancer. Must be unique per region per account, can have a maximum of 32 characters.
The IDs of the subnets. You can specify only one subnet per Availability Zone.
The IDs of the subnets. You can specify only one subnet per Availability Zone.
The IDs of the security groups for the load balancer
The IDs of the security groups for the load balancer

The scheme for the load balancer. An internet-facing load balancer routes requests from clients to targets over the internet. An internal load balancer routes requests to targets using private IP addresses.
The type of load balancer
The type of IP addresses used by the subnets

request.description: Creates an Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. For more information, see the User Guide for the specific load balancer type.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

The scheme for the load balancer. An internet-facing load balancer routes requests from clients to targets over the internet. An internal load balancer routes requests to targets using private IP addresses.
The type of load balancer
The type of IP addresses used by the subnets

item.name: ?Action=DeleteLoadBalancer
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Delete a Load Balancer

query.description: (Required) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the load balancer

request.description: Deletes the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. Deleting a load balancer also deletes its listeners.

item.name: ?Action=CreateTargetGroup
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Target Group

query.description: (Required) The name of the target group. Must be unique per region per account.
The protocol to use for routing traffic to the targets
The port on which the targets receive traffic
The identifier of the VPC for the targets
The protocol to use for health checks
The port to use for health checks
The destination for health checks on the targets
The type of target to register with the target group

request.description: Creates a target group. Target groups are used to route requests to one or more registered targets. After creating a target group, you can register targets and create listeners or rules to route traffic to the target group.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: ?Action=RegisterTargets
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Register Targets with a Target Group

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the target group
(Required) The ID of the target (instance ID, IP address, or Lambda ARN)
The port on which the target is listening
The Availability Zone where the target is located

request.description: Registers the specified targets with the specified target group. If the target is an EC2 instance, it must be in the running state when you register it.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: ?Action=DeregisterTargets
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Deregister Targets from a Target Group

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the target group
(Required) The ID of the target
The port on which the target is listening

request.description: Deregisters the specified targets from the specified target group. After the targets are deregistered, they no longer receive traffic from the load balancer.

item.name: ?Action=CreateListener
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Listener

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the load balancer
The protocol for connections from clients to the load balancer
(Required) The port on which the load balancer is listening
The security policy that defines which protocols and ciphers are supported
(Required) The type of action
The ARN of the target group for forward actions

request.description: Creates a listener for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. Listeners check for connection requests from clients using the protocol and port that you configure.

item.name: ?Action=CreateRule
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Listener Rule

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the listener
(Required) The rule priority. Must be unique within the listener.
The name of the field (host-header, path-pattern, etc.)
The condition values
The condition values
(Required) The type of action
The ARN of the target group for forward actions

request.description: Creates a rule for the specified listener. The listener must be associated with an Application Load Balancer. Rules are evaluated in priority order, from the lowest value to the highest value.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the listener
(Required) The rule priority. Must be unique within the listener.
The name of the field (host-header, path-pattern, etc.)
The condition values
(Required) The type of action
The ARN of the target group for forward actions

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the listener
